events.name: New York Jets at Cleveland Browns

headlines.description: — Everything was stacked against the New York Jets. The clock wasn't in their favor, and Cleveland's frothing home crowd was making things harder.
headlines.shortLinkText: Flacco rallies Jets to stunning 31-30 comeback over Browns

headlines.description: — The Detroit Lions waited until Week 13 of Dan Campbell's debut season to win last year.
headlines.shortLinkText: Goff throws 4 TD passes, Lions beat Commanders 36-27

events.name: Tampa Bay Buccaneers at New Orleans Saints

headlines.description: — It certainly looked like the Saints were in Tom Brady's head when the Buccaneers quarterback threw a tablet in the bench area and later jawed with New Orleans cornerback Marshon Lattimore after a drive-stalling incomplete pass.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brady's temper flares as Bucs snap skid vs Saints, 20-10

events.name: Carolina Panthers at New York Giants

headlines.description: — With Brian Daboll as their coach, the New York Giants are doing something they haven't done for more than five years: They're finding ways to win consistently.
headlines.shortLinkText: Giants 2-0 for 1st time in 6 years, beat Panthers 19-16

events.name: New England Patriots at Pittsburgh Steelers

headlines.description: — The New England Patriots don't panic. It doesn't matter the year, the opponent or even the quarterback.
headlines.shortLinkText: Patriots rely on defense to edge Watt-less Steelers 17-14

headlines.description: — Rayshawn Jenkins first sensed Jacksonville's defense would be different — dramatically improved, really — in May.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jags' home win streak vs Colts reaches 8 with 24-0 shutout

headlines.description: — Down by three touchdowns, Tua Tagovailoa and his speedy Miami receivers raced past the Baltimore Ravens in a stirring fourth-quarter comeback.
headlines.shortLinkText: Tagovailoa, Dolphins rally from 21 down to beat Ravens 42-38

events.name: Atlanta Falcons at Los Angeles Rams

headlines.description: — Jalen Ramsey was calm and collected, even in the maelstrom of the fourth quarter that saw the Atlanta Falcons putting together a late rally against the Los Angeles Rams.
headlines.shortLinkText: Stafford, Rams hold off Falcons for 31-27 victory

headlines.description: — After the shock of losing starting quarterback Trey Lance to a season-ending injury wore off, the San Francisco 49ers looked awfully familiar with Jimmy Garoppolo back at quarterback.
headlines.shortLinkText: Garoppolo comes off bench to lead 49ers past Seahawks 27-7

headlines.description: — Cooper Rush had no choice but to smile as he embraced offensive coordinator Kellen Moore after the winning kick for the Dallas Cowboys.
headlines.shortLinkText: Maher kick lifts Rush, Cowboys over Burrow, Bengals, 20-17

headlines.description: — Russell Wilson overcame a slow start and a cascade of boos in his Denver debut Sunday to lead the bungling Broncos past the Houston Texans 16-9 for rookie coach Nathaniel Hackett's first NFL win.
headlines.shortLinkText: Russell Wilson leads sloppy Broncos past Texans 16-9

events.name: Arizona Cardinals at Las Vegas Raiders

headlines.description: — The Cardinals' season appeared to be falling apart shortly after it began. One week after getting blown out by Kansas City, they took a 20-0 deficit into the locker room at halftime in Vegas.
headlines.shortLinkText: Murphy's 59-yard OT fumble return ends Cards' win over Vegas

events.name: Chicago Bears at Green Bay Packers

headlines.description: — Aaron Jones made sure the Green Bay Packers’ promise to get him the ball more often paid off.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rodgers, Packers lean on Jones, take care of Bears 27-10
